item.title: From Weak to Functional: Network Cooperativity and Anti-Cooperativity in Cyclic Neutral (HF)m(NH3)n Hydrogen Bonded Clusters
item.description: Individual hydrogen bond (HB) energies and cooperativity contributions were characterized across 66 HBs in twelve cyclic neutral (HF)m(NH3)n clusters (m + n = 3–7) using a molecular tailoring approach (MTA)-based fragmentation method at the MP2/aug-cc-...

item.description: Candidate structure prioritization in non-target screening (NTS) can be performed using collision cross-sections (CCSs) predicted by a machine-learning (ML) model, with a threshold on the relative difference between measured and in silico CCSs. Such ...

item.title: DNA-encoded chemical library-based identification of novel inhibitors targeting the essential LpxH enzyme in Gram-negative bacteria
item.description: LpxH is a key enzyme in the lipid A biosynthesis pathway and a promising target for the development of new antibiotics against Gram-negative pathogens. We report the discovery and optimization of two structurally distinct LpxH inhibitor series discovered ...

item.title: Ordering-dependent subsurface reconfiguration reshapes the ORR activity ranking of L10 and L12 Pt–Fe nanoparticles
item.description: Nanoparticle catalyst properties are often governed by activation-induced subsurface reconfiguration and ordering-dependent structural responses rather than nominal bulk phases. Static idealized models therefore can miss the kinetic pathways that drive ...

item.title: Harnessing Enzymatic Diazotization for Chemoenzymatic N–N Bond Formation and Diversification
item.description: Diazo compounds are highly versatile intermediates in synthetic chemistry, yet their selective biocatalytic formation remains rare. In nature, diazotases catalyze the ATP-dependent formation of diazo functionalities in the biosynthesis of natural ...

item.title: Pre-Analytical Integrity in Ignitable Liquid Residue Casework: Decision-Grade Reporting
item.description: Ignitable-liquid residue (ILR) analysis can provide powerful chemical evidence, but its forensic meaning depends on the integrity demonstrated before analysis. Volatile loss, alteration, contamination, transfer, background contribution, sampling ...

item.title: Improved and Simplified Treatment of Short-range Intramolecular Interactions in Polarizable Force Fields
item.description: Polarizable force fields such as AMOEBA provide a physically richer description of molecular interactions than fixed-charge models through permanent multipoles and explicit polarization with induced dipoles. However, the intramolecular treatment of ...

item.title: Coordination tuned Mn-based metal–ionic liquid frameworks for selective lignin catalysis
item.description: A coordination-tuned MnOH-[HEA][HCOO] metal–ionic liquid framework achieves 91% conversion of 1,4-dimethoxybenzene and 93% lignin conversion, demonstrating the catalytic advantage of distorted Mn–O sites for selective C–C/C–O bond cleavage. Metal–ionic ...

item.title: Metal-containing ionic liquids (Met-ILs) and metal–ionic-liquid frameworks (MIFs): synthesis, properties, and green-catalysis applications
item.description: Identifies benchmarking gaps for green-catalysis applications.

item.title: Text-mined dataset of sol-gel synthesis protocols with phase purity outcomes using large language models
item.description: Sol-gel synthesis is a versatile method for producing inorganic materials that offers advantages such as atomic-level mixing and lower processing temperatures over traditional solid-state methods. Despite these benefits, achieving phase purity remains a ...

item.title: Integrating Fragment Screening and Covalent Chemistry to Drug DNA-Binding Proteins: RAD52 as a Case Study
item.description: Protein-DNA interfaces are central to genome maintenance but remain challenging targets for small-molecule discovery because they are often broad, polar, solvent exposed, and lack classical druggable pockets. Here, we establish a structure-guided strategy ...

item.title: Lithium Makes the Difference–Evolution of Lithium Vacancy Disorder during Temperature-Induced Phase Transitions of the Li-Ion Conductor Li4SnS4
item.description: Sulfide-based lithium-ion solid electrolytes are promising candidates for solid-state batteries due to their high ionic conductivities, although they typically exhibit high moisture sensitivity. Li4SnS4has recently attracted scientific interest because ...

item.description: O-GalNAc glycans on glycoproteins are biologically important and structurally heterogeneous, varying in glycan composition and glycosylation sites. Clustered heterogeneous O-GalNAc glycans are key regulatory factors for immune regulation, cancer ...

item.description: Marine munition dumps continue to release munition compounds into coastal waters, but their fate cannot be inferred reliably from dissolved parent-compound concentrations alone. Parent loss may reflect transformation to measured or unmeasured products, ...

item.title: Light drives bidirectional two-electron transfer between paired dynamic redox molecules
item.description: We demonstrate closed photoredox systems in which light alone drives a net two-electron transfer between pairs of dynamic redox (dyrex) switches. The same pair can be cycled between two thermodynamically stable states using light of orthogonal wavelengths,...

item.description: This paper analyses the chemistry teacher education pathway at the University of Jyväskylä, examining how its course architecture systematically develops pedagogical content knowledge, inquiry‑ and phenomenon‑based pedagogy, student agency, and assessment ...

item.description: Constitutional isomerism is among the most fundamental forms of molecular structural variation, yet how it governs molecular recognition across multiple physicochemical scales remains largely unexplored. Here we establish a multiscale computational ...

item.description: Physical properties and toxicity of solvents remain barriers to scalable manufacturing of perovskite solar modules. In blade coating of thin films, solution viscosity and surface tension govern meniscus stability and wet-film quality. However, ...

item.title: Porous Organic Cages: CC1 as a Blueprint for Molecular Design, Structure–Property Relationships, and Future Perspectives
item.description: Porous materials such as inorganic porous materials, hybrid nanoparticles, metalorganic frameworks, porous organic polymers, metal organic polyhedral, and porous organic cages have attracted significant attention due to their diverse applications in gas ...

item.title: Total Synthesis of Piperhancin B and Isofutoquinol
item.description: We report the first total synthesis of piperhancin B, a natural neolignan exhibiting promising nitric oxide production inhibitory activity. Remarkably, this natural product possesses a highly strained housane structure, which constitutes a huge synthetic ...

item.title: Continuous UV-Spectroscopic Interrogation of Enzymes Processing Phosphorylated Intermediates Through Modular Reaction Networks
item.description: Phosphorylated intermediates are ubiquitous across primary and secondary metabolism, yet continuous and versatile assay systems for the interrogation of the enzymes that use them are underdeveloped. To address this gap, we developed a system forPhosphate ...

item.title: Photochemical Induction of Trion-based Polaritons in Quasi-1D Inorganic Nanotubes
item.description: Strong coupling between optical cavity modes and excitons in transition-metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) has attracted considerable interest due to its potential applications in nanophotonics and nonlinear optical technologies. Owing to their high refractive ...

item.title: Which state are you converging? A spin audit of sample-based quantum diagonalization benchmarks on iron–sulfur clusters
item.description: Sample-based quantum diagonalization (SQD/QSCI) selects determinant subspaces from quantum-circuit samples for classical diagonalization; its iron–sulfur demonstrations anchor utility-scale quantum-chemistry claims, and a recent critique disputes them at ...

item.title: Fluorescent Dyes in Image-Guided Surgery: Chemical Properties, Targeting, and Biomedical Applications
item.description: Fluorescence-guided surgery (FGS) has emerged as a transformative intraoperative modality that enables real-time, optical visualization of target tissues to improve surgical precision and oncological outcomes. This review examines the chemical properties, ...

item.title: Coumarin acrylamide as a fluorescence switch-on monomer to assay kinetics of radical polymerizations and for bioimaging
item.description: Radical polymerizations are among the most widely used polymer synthesis methods and have become versatile reactions for modifying living cells with bioorthogonal macromolecules and creating artificial cells. However, to follow and monitor these reactions,...

item.title: Mechanochemical synthesis of C-1 deuterated aldehydes and α-deutero acyloins via NHC catalysis
item.description: α-Deutero acyloins and C-1 deuterated aromatic aldehydes are synthesized via NHC catalysed umpolung using D2O under mechanochemical conditions. The competing aldehyde deuteration and deutero-benzoin condensation are controlled by the steric hindrance of ...

item.title: Measuring the Membrane Protein-Bound Lipidome
item.description: Lipids play diverse physiological roles in energy storage, signaling, and cell structure. Another less appreciated role is as allosteric modulators of membrane proteins. However, it is challenging to determine the identify and affinity of lipids ...

item.title: A Stereochemistry-Aware Circular Algorithm and Fingerprint for Resonance-Invariant Molecular Representation
item.description: Algorithmic molecular fingerprints remain attractive for high-throughput chemoinformatics because they are fast, discrete, and easy to integrate into screening, similarity search, and machine-learning workflows. However, widely used circular fingerprints ...

item.title: On the Role of Hydroxide Ions as the Mediators of Cathodic Cu Corrosion
item.description: Understanding changes and degradation of the copper cathode during the electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction reaction (CO2RR) is a fundamental challenge. Advances in operando experimental methods and computational tools have shed light on the ...

item.title: Trapping Spontaneously Formed Knots on Heterogeneously Charged Linear Polymers
item.description: Metastable knots in linear polymers are difficult to achieve because topological entanglements rapidly relax and untie. Using simulations of triblock copolyelectrolytes, we show that knots formed during poor-solvent collapse can be trapped by quenching to ...

item.title: Nonequilibrium Electronic Response Modes Govern Catalytic Energetics under Illumination
item.description: While nonequilibrium electronic configurations generated under illumination are widely recognized as central to photocatalysis, how they govern catalytic reaction energetics remains poorly understood. Here, we develop a delta self-consistent field (ΔSCF)-...

item.description: The quintet-to-singlet back-switching process in {[Fe(tpy)$_2$]$^{2+}$} (tpy=2,2’:6’,2"-terpyridine), {[Fe(dftpy)$_2$]$^{2+}$} (dftpy=6,6"-difluoro-2,2’:6’,2"-terpyridine) and {[Fe(dctpy)$_2$]$^{2+}$} (dctpy=6,6"-dichloro-2,2’:6’,2"-terpyridine), together ...

item.title: Finding the Roads Less Calculated in Organometallics with Automated Transition State Searches
item.description: Automated exploration of organometallic reaction mechanisms remains limited by the absence of molecular representations and enumeration rules that capture dative bonding, and by the computational cost of transition state refinement with large basis sets. ...

item.title: Raman Spectroscopic Characterization of the Benzene:Acetonitrile (3:1) Cocrystal Relevant to Titan, Saturn’s Moon
item.description: Titan, Saturn’s largest moon, hosts active organic chemistry and surface environments where small molecules are expected to crystallize as molecular minerals. Benzene and acetonitrile, both predicted to be present on Titan, were shown to form a ...

item.title: Exploration of BoltzGen cyclic-minipeptides targeting Ebola GP2 prefusion homotrimer-interfaces
item.description: Cyclic-minipeptide-conformer candidates targeted ebola virus (EBOV) GP2 prefusion homotrimer-interfaces implicated in viral fusion to host membranes. GP2 homotrimer, homodimer and αhelix interfaces were targeted by BoltzGen co-generating candidates ...

item.title: Analytical Theory of Topological Proton Glass: Dynamic Topological Constraints and the Quantum Topological Water Picture
item.description: We develop an analytical theoretical framework for a triple-helix closed-loop hydrogen-bond network to elucidate the coupling between geometric topological constraints and the statistical and dynamical behaviors of the system. The triple-helical closed-...

item.title: Access to diverse stacking motifs and colors in tetracyanoethylene charge transfer crystals with tetramethoxyanthracene donors
item.description: Organic charge transfer cocrystals (OCTCs) are an appealing platform for solution processable electronics with tunable properties. Thanks to their modular, molecular composition, OCTC electronics and optics can be varied by the design and selection of ...

item.title: Cheméo Relay, a D-MPNN-based chemical property prediction model family
item.description: A message-passing neural network is a learned group-contribution method only if its readout carries molecular size, which the framework-default mean readout discards. We train directed message passing neural networks (D-MPNN, Chemprop v2) on experimental ...

item.title: Free Ionomer Controls Adsorption in Electrolyzer Inks
item.description: The ionomer/catalyst interface in proton-exchange-membrane water-electrolyzers (PEMWEs) anode catalyst layers plays a pivotal role in governing multi-species transport and reaction kinetics. This interface originates from ionomer adsorption onto catalyst ...

item.title: MLIP-Enhanced Thermochemistry Predictions across Organic Chemical Space
item.description: This work presents thermoMLIP, a machine-learning-interatomic-potential-based workflow for efficient conformer optimization and thermochemistry prediction across organic chemical space. By combining MLIP-driven geometry optimization with robust ...

item.title: Quantum-Chemically Informed Representation Learning for Molecular Crystals Property Prediction via Multimodal Graph Neural Networks
item.description: Machine-learning based modeling of molecular crystals is limited by representations that encode geometric structure while overlooking the electronic features that often govern solid-state behavior. We introduce a multimodal learning framework that ...

item.title: Sulfonated nanoscale graphite as a catalyst for the alcoholysis of cellulose to ethyl levulinate
item.description: This study investigates the critical role of carbon catalyst surface architecture in the direct ("onepot") ethanolysis of microcrystalline cellulose to ethyl levulinate. Three sulfonated carbon matrices were synthesized and evaluated: pristine graphite, ...

item.title: Lamination Pressure Governs the Effectiveness of Sn Buffer Layers in Low-Pressure All-Solid-State Lithium Metal Batteries
item.description: All-solid-state lithium-metal batteries require stable Li plating/stripping under low stack pressures relevant to practical operation. Here, Sn was used as a model Li-alloying buffer layer to clarify how lamination pressure affects interfacial stability. ...

item.title: Deconstructing the distributional impacts of air pollution controls
item.description: Air quality policy increasingly seeks to reduce both population exposure and exposure disparities. For emissions-control policy, this requires identifying which sources, sectors, and locations have the greatest benefit per unit emissions. Here we develop ...

item.description: Predicting the most stable crystal structure of a given molecule remains a longstanding challenge. Crystal structure prediction (CSP) is the computational area of research seeking to identify all stable and metastable structures of a given compound using ...

item.description: Building reaction networks for kinetic modeling hinges on locating transition states (TSs) in order to compute reaction rates. In many cases, the reactions of interest involve complex, multi-step mechanisms with intermediates. Double-ended methods such as ...

item.description: Radical polymers are an emerging class of organic electronic materials that offer electronic properties, making them attractive for transparent, biosensing, and energy storage applications. Charge transport in these materials occurs through redox-active ...

item.description: The precise control of material properties through molecular-level architecture remains an enormous challenge in materials science. Here, we present a platform for the kinetic programming of covalently crosslinked networks by translating selective ...

item.description: Chemical pollution has emerged as a major threat to freshwater sustainability; however, its impacts and associated responsibilities across global supply chains remain insufficiently understood. This study developed an assessment framework to trace water ...

item.title: Planck Blackbody Radiation and Its Free Electron Model in Potential Box
item.description: Regarding the essential issue of the oscillator in Planck's blackbody thermal radiation, the free electron model (FEM) in potential box has been proposed. The results of nonlinear curve fitting have shown that FEM can describe the intensity distribution ...

item.description: Conformational strain, the energy a ligand pays to adopt its bound conformation, contributes to binding affinity but is routinely misestimated. The standard estimate references a single gasphase energy minimum, which ignores solvent and mishandles charged ...

item.description: Access to diverse, well-curated experimental data remains a major challenge in chemistry, underpinning applications ranging from machine learning (ML) and molecular discovery to data-driven scientific understanding. While several repositories provide ...

item.title: Foundation interatomic potentials learn the practical chemistry they were never taught
item.description: Chemists understand molecular reactivity through simplified concepts such as substituent effects, acidity, and aromaticity, which are compact descriptors of the potential energy surface and of the electronic structure that shapes it. Universal machine-...

item.title: Discrimination and Classification of Coke Variants via Hybrid E-tongue
item.description: Soft drinks contain different proportions of sugars, salts and other compounds, giving them a signature taste. We explore whether hybrid e-tongue (HET), utilized previously to discriminate pure tastes, can discern the tastes of Original (OC), Diet (DC) ...

item.title: Strategies Toward Pharmaceutical Self-reliance in Nepal for Strengthening the Health Care System.

item.title: Comparing Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory to Intermolecular Energy Decomposition Analysis Based on Absolutely Localized Molecular Orbitals
